section: General
text: Navigation Book & Prepare My bookings Discover Lufthansa 100 Years Lufthansa Navigation Book & Prepare My bookings Discover Lufthansa 100 Years Lufthansa Book & Prepare My bookings Discover Lufthansa 100 Years Lufthansa Start Assistance requirements Accessible travel Start Assistance requirements Accessible travel Start Assistance requirements Accessible travel Assistance requirements Accessible travel Services for travellers with reduced mobility or sensory or cognitive impairments We want you to feel safe, cared for and welcome at all times. Find out more here about our services for passengers with reduced mobility or sensory impairments for an accessible and comfortable journey. To make your time at the airport and your journey on board as comfortable as possible, we ask you to provide us with some information in advance. This allows us to meet your individual needs as best as possible. Please let us know what assistance you require no later than 48 hours before departure. This enables us to provide you with the best possible assistance. If you are travelling in a group with your own mobility aids, please notify us of your care requirements as early as possible.

section: General
text: If you register at short notice, we will, of course, strive for the best possible service, but cannot guarantee it. Without advance registration, neither the service nor travel on the booked flight can be guaranteed. Many airports offer their own services for passengers who require assistance. For details about these services, please check directly with the airports on your itinerary. You can tell us about your service needs in the following ways: By telephone If you book your ticket by telephone or through a travel agency, please be sure to inform us of your individual needs before completing your booking. Call 0800 8384267 , Please note that with this link you are visiting an external website that may not follow the same privacy, security or accessibility policies. Please note that with this link you are visiting an external website that may not follow the same privacy, security or accessibility policies. Online Our online service is available in German and English.

section: Lufthansa Medical Operation Centre
text: A companion is required if: You cannot understand the safety instructions from our cabin crew. You are unable to bring yourself to safety in the event of an emergency. You need personal assistance on board or in the lounge, e.g. For your safety and well-being during your trip, you may sometimes need to travel with a companion. A companion is required if: You cannot understand the safety instructions from our cab Statutory guidelines Passengers with reduced mobility are carried in accordance with EU Regulation No. 1107/2006 of 5 July 2006 on the rights of disabled passengers and passengers with reduced mobility. Any passengers travelling to/from the USA are carried in accordance with the U.S. DOT provisions, Title 14, Code of Federal Regulations, Part 382, effective as of 13 May 2009. At all our destinations in the USA as well as in Frankfurt and Munich, Complaint Resolution Officers (CRO) are at your service during opening hours, who you can contact directly in case of problems. Our crew members or ground staff will be happy to put you in contact.

Please note that with this link you are visiting an external website that may not follow the same privacy, security or accessibi Privacy settings We use cookies and similar technologies that are necessary to run the app and the website. Additional cookies are only used with your consent. We use them to access, analyse and store information such as the characteristics of your device as well as certain personal data (IP addresses, navigation usage, geolocation data or unique identifiers). The processing of your data serves various purposes: Analytics cookies allow us to analyse our performance to offer you a better online experience and evaluate the efficiency of our campaigns. Personalisation cookies give you access to a customised experience of our website with usage-based offers and support. Finally, Advertising cookies are placed by third-party companies processing your data to create audiences lists to deliver targeted ads on social media and the internet.

section: Find out more about our services for each type of impairment:
text: Services for passengers with reduced mobility Types of support we offer Depending on the degree to which your mobility is reduced, we offer the following services: WCHR: wheelchair assistance to the exit at the gate You can move independently on board the aircraft and go up and down stairs, but you need a wheelchair or other means of transport to travel long distances within the airport. WCHS: wheelchair assistance to the aircraft You can move independently on board the aircraft, but you can’t walk up or down stairs and need a wheelchair or another means of transport to get around the airport. WCHC: wheelchair assistance for a passenger who is unable to walk You are unable to walk, can only travel with your own wheelchair and require assistance from the moment you arrive at the airport, during all phases of boarding and disembarking, including onboard the aircraft, until the end of the flight and upon arrival at your destination. Travelling with your own wheelchair If you would like to take your own wheelchair with you, please note the following: At the airport, you will be provided with a wheelchair free of charge.

section: Find out more about our services for each type of impairment:
text: You can also transport your own (foldable) wheelchair in the cargo hold free of charge. If you need wheelchair assistance at your place of departure or destination, please make sure you inform us of this in good time, either during the booking process or afterwards. You can take up to two mobility aids (e.g. If you are taking your own wheelchair, please specify its dimensions, weight and special features (e.g. Please note that smaller aircraft may have size restrictions, e.g. A list of aircraft door dimensions by aircraft type can be found here: Aircraft door dimensions At most stations, you can use a foldable wheelchair until you reach the gate. section: Find out more about our services for each type of impairment:
text: WCHS: wheelchair assistance to the aircraft You can move independently on board the aircraft, but you can’t walk up or down stairs and need a wheelchair or another means of transport to get around the airport. WCHC: wheelchair assistance for a passenger who is unable to walk WCHC: wheelchair assistance for a passenger who is unable to walk You are unable to walk, can only travel with your own wheelchair and require assistance from the moment you arrive at the airport, during all phases of boarding and disembarking, including onboard the aircraft, until the end of the flight and upon arrival at your destination. At check-in, it will receive a "Delivery at aircraft" tag and will be loaded directly at the aircraft. In the case of battery-powered wheelchairs Travelling with your own wheelchair If you would like to take your own wheelchair with you, please note the following: At the airport, you will be provided with a wheelchair free of charge. In the case of battery-powered wheelchairs, there may be safety-related restrictions and certain regulations to follow. Please complete the form below and send it to specialcases@dlh.de so we can check whether transport is possible.

section: Find out more about our services for each type of impairment:
text: Booking and reservation In principle, you are free to choose your seat according to your individual needs. Please understand that, for safety reasons, restrictions may apply in some cases as follows: For all seats in the emergency exit row, regardless of aircraft type B747-400 and B747-800: seats on the upper deck are not suitable for WCHS and WCHC as the upper deck can only be reached via a staircase. If you are dependent on a mobility aid and are travelling in the new Allegris cabin on an A350-900, please note the following instructions regarding seat selection: First Class: 1K is not suitable for WCHC. Business Class: Business Suites 2A-K and 8A-K, Privacy Seats 3A+K, 5A+K, 7A+K, 9A+K and 11A+K as well as all Extra Space Seats 3E, 5F, 9E and 11F are not suitable for WCHC. Economy Class: rows 19-K, 26A-C and 26H-K are not suitable for WCHC, WCHS and WCHR. Learn more about aircraft specifications and onboard mobility. Almost all seats on the Lufthansa fleet have movable armrests which make access to your seat easier. If you wish to attach a seating device to your seat, then for safety reasons we must check the device beforehand.

section: Find out more about our services for each type of impairment:
text: In this case, please contact our ​ Medical Operation Centre . You can register a battery-powered wheelchair here. In the event of irregularities with your mobility aid Lufthansa undertakes to handle your wheelchair or electric mobility scooter (hereinafter referred to as "mobility aid") with care. Despite our efforts, mobility aids may be damaged or delayed. If your mobility aid arrives late or has been damaged during the trip, please report this immediately, either online or directly at the lost-property office at the destination airport. We will work to find a solution as quickly as possible. ​Create a report The link will be opened in a new browser tab , Please note that with this link you are visiting an external website that may not follow the same privacy, security or accessibility policies. ​Create a report The link will be opened in a new browser tab , Please note that with this link you are vis If your mobility aid arrives late, you can choose the option that suits you best: you can either collect the mobility aid at the destination airport or have it conveniently delivered to a suitable location, such as your home or your hotel.

section: Find out more about our services for each type of impairment:
text: If you incur transport costs to or from the airport due to your mobility aid being delayed, we will of course reimburse you for these. Please note that we require proof of the expenses incurred. Devices on loan If your mobility aid is damaged during the flight, you are entitled to a loan wheelchair that best meets your requirements in terms of functionality and safety until your own device has been returned, repaired or replaced. If we are unable to provide a suitable mobility aid on loan, you can also organise one yourself. We will reimburse you for the resulting costs within 30 days after submission of the corresponding proof. If your mobility aid is damaged during the flight, you are entitled to a loan wheelchair that best meets your requirements in terms of functionality and safety until your own device has been returned, repaired or replaced. Repair or replacement In the event of a damaged or lost mobility aid, you will be offered the following options: Lufthansa will cover the costs of repairing or replacing the device and will offer you a device with comparable functionality and safety.

section: Hidden Disabilities Sunflower
text: Booking and reservation You can register your support requirements online here: Go to the Service Centre Find out more here about how to register an assistance dog: Information about taking assistance dogs with you At the airport We will be happy to accompany you throughout your time at the airport if needed. At the gate, we can assist you with boarding and disembarking before the other passengers. If required, we will provide you with a separate briefing in the event of delays and other important travel announcements. View the check-in information On board Our cabin crew will give you separate, personal safety instructions. Our crew will also provide additional helpful information, such as for your orientation in the cabin. Our onboard team will also explain how your food is arranged on the tray so that you have a clear idea of how your meal is presented. To destination airport Our staff will also be happy to help you at your destination or if you need to change flights. They will accompany you to the airport exit or look after you until your connecting flight.

text: Book & Prepare My bookings Discover Lufthansa 100 Years Lufthansa Start Baggage Travelling with animals Assistance dogs Start Baggage Travelling with animals Assistance dogs Start Baggage Travelling with animals Assistance dogs Travelling with animals Assistance dogs Regardless of its breed or type, an assistance dog is specially trained to take on tasks or work for a person with an impairment. These may include visual, sensory, psychiatric, intellectual or other cognitive impairments. You can take an assistance dog with you on any route free of charge. However, certain requirements must be met for you to take a dog into the cabin: In order to ensure the safe and undisturbed operation of the flight, your dog must be trained to obey your commands and must behave appropriately in a public space (it must not bark, growl or jump up at people or other animals). Your dog must never defecate in an uncontrolled manner in the cabin or at the gate. If it is necessary, it must be done in a way that does not affect the hygiene or health of others.

section: Hidden Disabilities Sunflower
text: If your dog does not behave in an appropriate manner, Lufthansa reserves the right to transport the dog in the cargo hold for a surcharge or to refuse to carry the animal. You are liable for any damage and additional costs arising from the carriage of your dog. You have the following options for registering your assistance dog: Register your assistance dog online Email the Lufthansa Medical Operation Centre The link will be opened in a new browser tab ​Email the Lufthansa Medical Operation Centre in the USA The link will be opened in a new browser tab ​ Via the Help Centre Through your travel agency In addition, the general advice for the carriage of animals in aircraft applies when you take an assistance dog with you into the cabin. General advice for the carriage of animals in aircraft We distinguish between Assistance dogs on all flights outside the United States Assistance dogs on non-stop flights to/from the United States You have made sure that carrying your dog with you on your journey (outbound and return flight) as additional carry-on baggage in the cabin complies with the current entry and exit regulations of all the countries concerned.

section: You have the following options for registering your assistance dog:
text: General advice for the carriage of animals in aircraft We distinguish between Assistance dogs on all flights outside the United States Assistance dogs on non-stop flights to/from the United States In addition, the general advice for the carriage of animals in aircraft applies when you take an assistance dog with you into the cabin. General advice for the c Ideally, please register your dog for transport in the cabin no later than 48 hours before departure so that we can prepare everything optimally. We also ask you to provide us with the following documents: The training certificate for your assistance dog: A training certificate from a training institution that holds accreditation from the following organisations: Messerli Research Institute Assistance Dogs International (ADI) Assistance Dogs Europe (ADEu) International Guide Dog Federation (IGDF) or A certificate issued by a recognised training facility or a qualified dog trainer (i.e. In addition, the dog must wear a clearly visible identification tag, e.g. Please note that a certificate of completed obedience training or the completed DOT Form is not sufficient for travel outside the USA.

section: You have the following options for registering your assistance dog:
text: Book & Prepare My bookings Discover Lufthansa 100 Years Lufthansa Start Assistance requirements Accessible travel Passengers with sensory impairment Start Assistance requirements Accessible travel Passengers with sensory impairment Start Assistance requirements Accessible travel Passengers with sensory impairment Assistance requirements Passengers with sensory impairment Passengers with sensory impairment Lufthansa provides special services for passengers with hearing or visual impairments or with cognitive disabilities before, during and after the flight. Booking and reservation When you book your trip, you can help us to provide the service you need on the ground and during your flight. We require some advance information from you so that we can ensure your comfort at the airport and on board our aircraft. Please let us know by telephone what assistance you require. You can also register your assistance requirements after booking online. When booking tickets by telephone or at a travel agency, please indicate your needs before completing the booking. Please let us know what assistance you require at least 48 hours before departure to ensure you receive the best support.

section: You have the following options for registering your assistance dog:
text: We can no longer guarantee the best assistance and support if you register at shorter notice. Register your assistance requirements online The link will be opened in a new browser tab Register your assistance requirements by calling tel.: 0800 - 83 84 267 The link will be opened in a new browser tab , Please note that with this link you are visiting an external website that may not follow the same privacy, security or accessibility policies. Please note that, in principle, you are free to choose the seat that best meets your requirements. Please note, however, that for safety reasons there may be restrictions on your choice. Almost all seats on the Lufthansa fleet have moveable armrests which make access to your seat easier. If you wish to use a seating device on your seat, then for safety reasons we must check the device beforehand. In such cases, please contact our Medical Operation Centre. In addition, we can transport up to two mobility aids (e.g. Passengers with sensory impairment Lufthansa provides special services for passengers with hearing or visual impairments or with cognitive disabilities before, during and after the flight. Services in detail As a visua

section: You have the following options for registering your assistance dog:
text: When you book your trip, you can help us to provide the service you need on the ground and during your flight. Services in detail As a visually impaired passenger you do not need to have an assessment of your fitness for air travel. We offer visually impaired passengers the following assistance: Separate briefing in the event of delays and other important travel announcements Other assistance, such as being escorted at the airport Separate, individual safety briefings Explanations of the arrangement of food on the meal tray Our staff will also be happy to help you at your destination or if you need to change flights. For longer stopovers in Frankfurt or Munich we have set up a waiting lounge and can take visually impaired passengers there. We offer the following assistance to hearing-impaired passengers: Separate briefing in the event of delays and other important travel announcements Separate, individual safety briefings Films with German and English subtitles Our staff will also be happy to help you at your destination or if you need to change flights.

section: You have the following options for registering your assistance dog:
text: For longer stopovers in Frankfurt or Munich we have set up a waiting lounge and can take hearing-impaired passengers there. In principle, Lufthansa carries passengers with a cognitive impairment. However, it is important to note the following points: The routes through international airports are often long and complicated and passengers may have difficulty finding their way around and communicating. Security, customs and police checkpoints may cause stress and anxiety. It is absolutely imperative that the passenger is able to follow the crew’s safety instructions during the flight, in the event of turbulence. The coping abilities of a person with a cognitive impairment should not be overestimated. In the case of specific impairments which lead to confusion or disorientation (e.g. Alzheimer’s), there is a risk that the passenger will not pay attention to the safet However, certain requirements must be met in order for you to take your dog into the cabin. We have summarised all the information about the carriage of assistance dogs for you on the following page.
